item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This already started strong and near-instantly garnered a +1 out of me, but the journal entry detailing the Fool’s encounter with possibly the Foundation years prior very much begs its own tale, and would love to see how he managed to get so close without getting amnesticized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading the journal entries, I think you should change the time period so it's not set in the 50's as several things mentioned throughout don't make sense for that time period both in terms of events at the time as well as events being talked about having happened even earlier in time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coolio. Thanks a lot for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having the old man be a bit tense around criminals since he seems to instantly know people's past was clever, great scp all around- upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neato. That's really cool and nice to hear. Thanks man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An old man who paints so that people would be able to go beyond their own grief. This SCP reminds me of SCP-948, one of my favorites. It's nice to see non-spooky ghosts for a change. Paranormal doesn't have to be scary, as long as there's emotion. +1, very touching

item.title: Hey, I Wrote a Thing
item.content:encoded:
Yep. Also, that redacted SCP in the testing logs is probably up for crosslinking if I ever get the courage to ask someone about using their SCP or whatever. So yeah. Huge thanks to these people for the feedback during the brainstorming and drafting parts:

Modern-Erasmus

ChrizC (No link, can't find that profile)

Countless-Skies

Voct (No link again, whoops)

Bridge Photo was authored by Darren Wyn Rees and was released to the public domain. Here's link to the original: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:TrecynonIronBridge.jpg
